title: Build the controlled classroom fixture

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.name: Missing deterministic controlled-fixture domain
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/controlled-fixture.test.ts --project property tests/property/controlled-fixture.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: All five examples and generated invariants failed because the nine-version scenario builder and submission planner did not exist

commands.name: Fixture examples and seeded PROP-23/PROP-24 invariants
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/controlled-fixture.test.ts --project property tests/property/controlled-fixture.property.test.ts

commands.name: Missing fixture HTTP and isolation boundary
commands.expectedFailure: All three initial integration scenarios reached the not-yet-created startFixtureServer boundary

commands.name: Public scenario, DPA, collector, failure, and hidden-oracle HTTP boundary
commands.command: pnpm --filter @pactwire/fixture typecheck; pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/controlled-fixture.test.ts

commands.name: Undefined controlled-fixture browser stories
commands.expectedFailure: All twenty-one inherited setup steps passed while nine controlled-fixture scenarios and fifty-four new behavior steps remained undefined

commands.name: Fixture prompt story inherited unrelated console background requests
commands.expectedFailure: Eight fixture scenarios passed; the prompt story inherited a Pactwire-console background whose in-flight canary reads completed after navigation, so the harness correctly reported three unexpected 404 responses

commands.name: Independent-oracle alignment and focused acceptance
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/controlled-fixture.test.ts --project property tests/property/controlled-fixture.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/controlled-fixture.test.ts

commands.name: Evidence index rejected stale manifest and proof totals
commands.expectedFailure: The evidence-harness regression still expected eleven manifests and 107 proof files after FIX-01 added the twelfth manifest and fifteen referenced proofs

commands.name: Evidence index includes the controlled fixture manifest and proofs
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts; pnpm evidence:check

commands.name: Strict full-suite type check required explicit dispatch narrowing
commands.expectedFailure: All static gates before type checking passed; noUncheckedIndexedAccess required the integration test to prove both regression dispatches existed after its runtime length assertion

commands.name: Built CLI health smoke
commands.command: PACTWIRE_FIXTURE_PORT=4117 PACTWIRE_FIXTURE_VERSION=BASELINE node apps/fixture/dist/cli.js; GET http://127.0.0.1:4117/health

commands.name: Workspace credential-pattern scan
commands.command: rg -P with token-boundary secret-key and private-key patterns, excluding dependencies, build output, artifacts, and images; no matches is success

scenarios.scenario: The baseline teacher and student journeys produce only allowed first-party traffic
Interface drift moves the student checkpoint without changing the seeded traffic
Regression mode sends the exact synthetic student email to fixture analytics
Repaired mode sends analytics only an aggregate completion event
Ambiguous mode sends an opaque reference to an unknown destination
Invisible mode marks the required submission request unavailable
The controlled fixture labels an injected instruction without changing the authorized traffic
Failure mode returns a visible outage and dispatches no request
Risky-action mode stops before messaging a real person

visualEvidence.rationale: Eleven development captures were reviewed; curated optimized-production copies will be attached after the implementation source commit exists.

proofs.description: Fictional-only scenario, exact DPA, nine version plans, ambiguity, visibility, interface drift, prompt injection, risky action, and outage examples
Seeded PROP-23 reproducibility and PROP-24 declared-version isolation over generated fixture seeds
HTTP, reserved destinations, collector ledger, DPA asset, hidden-oracle isolation and alignment, risky stop, outage, and visibility proof
Repository evidence index includes the FIX-01 manifest and every referenced proof without orphans
Exact student-email regression to fictional analytics
Unknown destination with unsupported opaque transform
Untrusted instruction remains page content and cannot change seeded traffic
Seeded submission outage and real-person risky-action stop
Playwright journeys, reserved-host traffic assertions, independent hidden-manifest checks, and visual capture
Deterministic scenario domain, traffic plans, hardened HTTP server, fictional UI, collectors, and event ledger
Non-exported independent oracle for journey, bounded result, destination, canary, transform, visibility, and real-world-action facts
Fictional source agreement with exact allowed and prohibited recipient language
Local controlled-fixture launcher with explicit mode, seed, address, and Chromium host mapping

knownLimitations: A controlled fixture proves seeded mechanism behavior only; it is not evidence about a real vendor, legal meaning, safety, compliance, adoption, or market impact.
The oracle is absent from package exports and HTTP routes, but repository administrators can read its file; blinded evaluations must withhold it from the application evaluator.
Reserved destination hosts require the documented controlled-Chromium resolver rule and are not public DNS names.
The fixture records actual seeded requests in memory; RUN-02 owns the independent recorder, DET-03 owns findings, and DET-05 owns approval holds.
Fixture process state is intentionally ephemeral and resets on restart.
